channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Averyhill park burnt out motor bike, 26th June
item.description:
Burnt out motor bike back of averyhill park. On pathway/cycle way. Field that backs on to charlton athletic practice ground.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sparrows Lane, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown base of tree obstructing crossing and passing, 26th June
item.description:
Overgrown public tree that makes it very hard to cross the road as you cannot see oncoming vehicles there is also little to no access for a wheelchair user or someone in a pram.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Burnt Ash Lane, Sundridge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Four defects, please see below, 26th June
item.description:
There are four defects in the road surface in Burnt Ash Lane around the Junction with Treewall Gardens which are impacting on residents in the vicinity. Before detailing the defects, we have noticed that two of the three sections of Burnt Ash Lane have been resurfaced in recent times. We refer to the sections between Plaistow Green and the Shops and then the section with the shops through to the Toyota Garage. We had hoped that the final section, through to Ridgeway Drive would have now been completed. If that resurfacing is planned in the near future, please let us know when. If there are no plans to resurface this section of the roadway then please take action to resolve the following. The four defects being reported are the sites of old road works which have been previously repaired and are failing again. One repair is subsiding, another has numerous cracks. In addition, there is a circular hole in the road surface which is disintegrating and exposing the surface below. Finally, there is a relatively new crack running right across the surface, to the entrance of Treewall Gardens. The faults are becoming progressively worse, largely due the significant and increasing number of large HGV’s using Burnt Ash Lane. We appreciate that repairs can be costly, but if the final section of Burnt Ash Lane is not due to be resurfaced, could you please look at the required repairs as ‘a stitch in time will save nine’. Finally, the deteriorating road surface does impact on those of us living adjacent to the same, as large vehicles tend to ‘bounce’ on the road defects causing both vibration and noise. Thank you in anticipation of your attention to this matter.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Burnt Ash Lane, Sundridge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead parrot on pavement outside 59 ormiston road, 26th June
item.description:
Dead parrot showed up today (Friday) evening outside 59 Ormiston Road.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Ormiston Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

On pavement

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lea Bridge Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Street Light Not Working – Malpas Road E8 (No. 10), 26th June
item.description:
I would like to report a street light that has not been working for approximately 8 months. Location: Outside Number 10, Malpas Road, E8, London The street light has been non-functional for a long period, causing poor visibility and safety concerns for r

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Malpas Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Plastic stuck in the tree, 26th June
item.description:
This plastic has been stuck in the tree for some time

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Chelmer Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Rubbish dump again at start of Langley rd, 26th June
item.description:
Rubbish again dumped
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Langley Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Garden waste not collected, 26th June
item.description:
Garden waste has not been collected Bin sticker for 2026/27 subscription has not been delivered one month after being paid for Emails and reports to council remain unanswered
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 75, Adelaide Avenue, Ladywell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bikes and scooters badly parked, 26th June
item.description:
Lime and Forest bikes/scooters are being left constantly in a pedestrian area in the main entry, maintenance entry and surrounding area of Vista Heights (SE15 1PP) for months. The big planters were placed to avoid bikes, scooters and even a motorbike being parked in there but the issue persist and has even worsened. The pavement area is not purposed to leave bikes in there please. Note wheelchair users and people with prams have difficulties moving around with so many bikes left everywhere and blocking the entries. I already reported it to Lime and Forest. Attentive to your reply. Best regards.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 227-255, Ilderton Road, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flint gravel escaping driveway onto carriageway, 26th June
item.description:
There is a recurring issue with large flint gravel escaping from a private driveway onto the carriageway. The stones are typically 20-30 mm in diameter and regularly accumulate in the road. As a cyclist I have twice almost lost control after riding over them. This is a recurring highway safety hazard rather than a one-off incident. Please could the carriageway be inspected and the source addressed to prevent further debris entering the road?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 32A, Mayplace Road East, Barnehurst, Bexleyheath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped rubbish, 26th June
item.description:
Dumped rubbish saw them coming out of a house on Castlecombe Road and walked it there and dumped it

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beaconsfield Road, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Small fridge dumped on pavement, 26th June
item.description:
Small fridge dumped on pavement. Been there for at least a week or two

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 133-135, Widmore Road, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree growth covering most of the pavement, 26th June
item.description:
A tree is growing from its base and covering most of the pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 46, Welling Way, Falconwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trees were recently cut... trees over growing on to pavement making it unaccessible, 26th June
item.description:
I had a friend visit recently with two young children 1 over 5 the other just 6months. Parking in the designated area she struggled to get hwr child out and away from the leaves poking out of the branches. We had to use the drive through road to place baby in car seat/pram etc. A single pran yet alone a individual person is unable to walk on this pathway. Also the trees have maggots hanging from them yellow insects. My son suffers wuth eczema and has documented evidence of allergy to trees and pollen. He often rodes his bike on the estate near our home I caught my young child entering the road to het to the park access the other day as branches and leaves are very itchy! He tried to avoid such.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Glazebrook Close, Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Huge pot hole, 26th June
item.description:
There has been a huge pot hole here for months, people swerve left and right to avoid going over it.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 95, Halfway Street, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing bins at Sara Lane Court, 26th June
item.description:
The bins weren’t collected earlier in the week, and the bins for the flats are overflowing. They’re also directly beneath the building’s main gas supply which, even though it has recently been upgraded, doesn’t feel particularly safe.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Stanway Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lifts are inoperable, 26th June
item.description:
Both.lifts in eckington house fladbury road have broken down
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 21-25, Fladbury Road, West Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cardboard boxes have been dumped outside block 13-20 Jubilee Way, 26th June
item.description:
The boxes appeared in beginning of the week in front of the block 13-20- however you can see who they belong to as had a address sticker visible . They have also overflown the communal bin storage with household items. Is evident that this is no one from our block and we are now unable to dispose our bins.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Jubilee Way, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
